Public skating times change monthly, so use the Ice Arena Google Calendar and the public skating page to confirm the exact session times before you go.

seo.long_description:
Across from Centennial Park, Centennial Sportsplex Ice Arenas in Nashville, TN is a year-round ice center with two full-size sheets. The rink lists each sheet at 200 feet by 85 feet, which is helpful if you are comparing rink dimensions for hockey or figure skating.

Session times are not fixed week to week. The rink points skaters to the Centennial Sportsplex Ice Arena Google Calendar, and the public skating page links you back to that calendar. If you are planning a visit, check the event titles on the calendar to confirm whether a slot is public skating, hockey, or a special program.

On-site amenities listed by the rink include skate rental, spectator seating, lockers, a concession stand, and and skate sharpening. Learn-to-skate and figure skating training is offered through Nashville Skating Academy, and adult open hockey and stick and puck details are outlined on the official hockey program page.
helpful_blurbs: Trying to plan around a busy week in Nashville? Start with the Ice Arena Google Calendar, since public skating and hockey sessions are posted and adjusted by month.

If you are working on fundamentals, the Learn To Ice Skate page points you to Nashville Skating Academy for learn-to-skate and figure skating training. That is a good path if you want structured progression instead of only public sessions.
Hockey players can use the adult open hockey page to understand age limits, required gear, and how sessions are capped. Then jump to the calendar to find the next date that fits your schedule.
Need a tune-up before your next session? Explore skate sharpening options and turnaround notes, and browse other Tennessee rinks to compare services and amenities.
Looking for focused practice ice? Check the freestyle hub for how to search calendars, then verify whether Centennial’s calendar lists freestyle-style sessions for your target week.
what_to_know: The facility lists two full-size ice rinks with sheets measuring 200 feet by 85 feet.
Public skating sessions vary by day and month, so the rink recommends using the Ice Arena Google Calendar to confirm times.
The public skating rates page states that skate rental is included with admission for public sessions.
Lessons are offered through Nashville Skating Academy, and the Learn To Ice Skate page lists recurring class times for their seven-week series.
The adult open hockey page lists a posted session price and notes that schedules are published on the Ice Arena Google Calendar.
Adult open hockey and stick and puck sessions have published rules, including required protective gear and no checking.
Skate sharpening is available for figure and hockey skates, with the rink asking you to allow about 24 hours for completion.
Amenities listed on the ice skating page include spectator seating, lockers, and a concession stand.
The rink address is 222 25th Ave. N., Nashville, TN 37203, and the Sportsplex is described as directly across the street from Centennial Park.
